name: Sierra de Sena Loop via El Pilón and
name_original: El Pilón, Monasterio de Sigena, y el refugio de Piedrahita — circular por la Sierra de Sena
description: This moderate 21.1-mile mountain bike route in Sierra de Sena visits El Pilón, Sijena Monastery, and Refugio de Piedrahita.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Sigena traverse a unique semi-desert landscape in the Los Monegros county of Huesca, Aragon, Spain. The region is characterized by arid terrain, striking ochre colors, and diverse natural features. Mountain bikers can expect rugged and varied topography, including curious rock formations known as Tozales and the Sierra de Sena mountain range, which offers significant elevation. Best mountain bike trails around Villanueva De Sigena The most popular mountain bike trail is Sariñena, Tozales and El Pilón - circular route through the Monegros, a 43.0 miles (69.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 44 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the arid landscapes and unique geological formations of Los Monegros, including the Tozales and El Pilón. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Sierra de Sena Loop via El Pilón and, a moderate 21.1 miles (33.9 km) path. This route offers varied terrain through the Sierra de Sena mountain range, featuring climbs to El Pilón. Local mountain bikers also love the Sigena Monastery / Sixena – Sijena Monastery loop from Sena, a 34.3 miles (55.3 km) trail leading through the Monegros landscape, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Mountain biking around Villanueva De Sigena is defined by the Monegros desert landscape, unique Tozales rock formations, and the Sierra de Sena mountain range. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging climbs. The routes in Villanueva De Sigena are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 40 reviews. More than 2800 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Sigena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Villanueva de Sijena?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Villanueva de Sijena, offering a variety of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 2800 times.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Villanueva de Sijena?
A: The terrain around Villanueva de Sijena is characterized by a unique semi-desert landscape within the Los Monegros county. You can expect arid, rugged, and varied topography, including striking ochre-colored formations known as Tozales and the significant climbs of the Sierra de Sena mountain range. Trails often feature open slopes and technical sections.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails for beginners in Villanueva de Sijena?
A: Yes, there are a couple of easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sariñena loop from Sariñena&lt;/a&gt; is an easy route covering just over 20 km with minimal elevation gain, perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can tackle routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1147801825/sarinena-tozales-and-el-pilon-circular-route-through-the-monegros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sariñena, Tozales and El Pilón - circular route through the Monegros&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 69 km trail explores arid landscapes and unique geological formations, including significant climbs to El Pilón.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Villanueva de Sijena are circular. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e964847657/sierra-de-sena-loop-via-el-pilon-and" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Sena Loop via El Pilón and&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 34 km and offers varied terrain and climbs. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigena Monastery / Sixena – Sijena Monastery loop from Sena&lt;/a&gt;, a 55 km route through the Monegros landscape.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails offer access to several unique natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can encounter the curious rock formations known as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5228497" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozales de Monegros&lt;/a&gt;, or the striking &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771760" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tozal de la Cobeta&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, routes often pass by &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105057" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit)&lt;/a&gt;, which is the highest point of the Sierra de Sena and offers vistas across the province of Huesca.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural attractions near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. The Royal Monastery of Santa María de Sijena, a significant 12th-century site blending Romanesque and Gothic styles, is often a point of interest on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38166054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sigena Monastery / Sixena – Sijena Monastery loop from Sena&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find the Michael Servetus Interpretation Center in Villanueva de Sijena itself.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Villanueva de Sijena?
A: The Monegros region can experience extreme temperatures. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions for mountain biking, with milder weather. Summers can be very hot due to the semi-desert climate, and winters can be chilly, though riding is possible year-round with appropriate preparation.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Villanueva de Sijena?
A: The mountain bike trails in Villanueva de Sijena are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars. Reviewers often praise the unique, otherworldly semi-desert landscape, the challenging and varied terrain, and the sense of solitude and adventure found on these routes.

Q: Are there any facilities like refuges or huts along the trails?
A: Yes, some routes may pass by facilities such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2105056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Refugio Piedrafita&lt;/a&gt;. This stone refuge offers a place to rest with tables, benches, and a fireplace, though access may require prior contact with the local Town Hall.

Q: Is public transport available to Villanueva de Sijena for cyclists?
A: While Villanueva de Sijena is accessible by road, specific public transport options designed for cyclists with bikes may be limited. It's advisable to check local bus or train schedules in advance for services that accommodate bicycles, especially if traveling from larger nearby cities.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Villanueva de Sijena area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on the established trails around Villanueva de Sijena. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, especially when riding through natural protected areas or private land. Always stick to marked trails.
tips: It is a small stone shelter typical of the Monegros region, designed to provide shelter for shepherds or hikers in bad weather.

It's a small stone monolith that marks one of the highest points of the route, and although it's not very large, everyone who passes by instantly recognizes it.

After kilometers of open slopes and wind (sometimes very strong), reaching the Pilón is like a symbol that you've overcome most of your hard work. Many take the opportunity to make a brief stop, take a quick photo, and catch their breath before continuing.

Its construction, from the 12th century, was ordered by Sancha de Castilla for the hospital sisters. Therefore, even though it was a double monastery (with a part for nuns and another for friars), the highest authority was always in the hands of the nuns, who at various times were women linked to royalty or the Aragonese nobility.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty and terrain like for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This is a moderate mountain bike route, approximately 33.9 kilometers long with an elevation gain of 608 meters. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. The terrain is varied, featuring open tracks that can be subject to strong winds, and potentially some technical sections. While not overly technical, it presents a good challenge for experienced riders.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers diverse scenery and significant landmarks. You will pass by the historic Sijena Monastery, reach El Pilón (Sierra de Sena Summit), a stone monolith marking one of the highest points, and visit the Refugio Piedrafita, a well-equipped stone refuge with excellent views.

faqs.question: Where can I park for this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The provided data does not specify a designated parking area. However, as a circular route, you would typically start and end at the same point. It is advisable to check local regulations or Komoot's community comments for common starting points and parking availability near the beginning of the trail, such as close to the Sijena Monastery.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this route?
faqs.answer: There is no information indicating that permits or entrance fees are required to ride this mountain bike route. Access to the natural areas and the trail itself is generally open.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The best time of year to ride this route would generally be during spring or autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot in this region, and strong winds can be a factor on the open tracks, especially around El Pilón. Always check the weather forecast before heading out.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The information available does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed on this specific mountain bike route. When riding with dogs, it's always best to keep them on a leash for their safety and to respect wildlife, especially in natural areas. Consider the trail's moderate difficulty and length when deciding if it's suitable for your dog.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Refugio Piedrafita, a notable stop on the route, is generally equipped with potable water. This makes it a convenient and luxurious spot for a stop to refill your bottles.

faqs.question: What equipment should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the route's moderate difficulty and varied terrain, a full-suspension mountain bike is recommended for comfort and capability. Essential gear includes a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, sufficient water (even with the refuge stop), snacks, a repair kit, and navigation tools. Be prepared for potential strong winds, especially on exposed sections.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or out-and-back?
faqs.answer: This is a circular route, meaning it is a loop trail. You will start and end at the same point, making it convenient for parking and logistics.

faqs.question: Are there any specific challenges or conditions to be aware of on this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, riders should be aware that the route is rated as moderate and requires good fitness and advanced riding skills. The open tracks, particularly on the ascent to El Pilón, can be exposed to strong winds. While not overly technical, some sections may present challenges typical of mountain biking in the region, potentially including technical descents or rocky areas.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Due to its moderate difficulty rating, length of 33.9 km, and elevation gain of 608 meters, this route is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires good fitness and advanced mountain biking skills. More experienced riders will find it a rewarding challenge.
